fresh_facts.claim: Israel conducted extensive strikes across Iran on March 15-16, 2026, targeting over 200 locations including nuclear program infrastructure and IRGC/Basij headquarters.
Israeli strikes destroyed more than 85% of Iran's air defense systems and radars on March 15-16, 2026.
Israeli strikes targeted over 100 Iranian production and armament facilities and more than 500 command and control targets on March 15-16, 2026.
The Supreme Leader's aircraft was destroyed at Mehrabad Airport in Tehran during Israeli strikes on March 15-16, 2026.
Israel assassinated Ali Larijani, Iran's security chief, and Gholamreza Soleimani, commander of the Basij paramilitary force, on March 17, 2026.
Iran's missile launch capacity declined significantly by March 17, 2026, compared to earlier peaks in the campaign.
A US refueling aircraft crashed in western Iraq on March 13, 2026, killing six crew members after being struck by Iranian missiles.
An IRGC commander stated on March 10, 2026 that Iran was shifting to firing only missiles with 1,000 kg or larger payloads, moving from saturation tactics to high-impact penetration strikes.
Ordinary Iranian citizens provided targeting intelligence to Israeli and US forces by filming Basij positions and posting on social media in real time.
Over 5,000 total targets have been struck across Iran since the start of the campaign as of March 17, 2026.
Israeli and US strikes targeted detention facilities in Kurdish areas including Mariwan, resulting in a reported jailbreak.
The IRGC ordered evacuation of Mariwan on March 3, 2026, potentially indicating preparations for ground confrontation in the border region.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed in a U.S. airstrike according to reported developments.
Following the Supreme Leader's death, Mojtaba Khamenei's reported inner circle is dominated by long-standing hardline IRGC commanders.
Abu Zar Hejazi and other Basij commanders were killed or targeted on March 17, 2026.
Israeli and US strikes targeted Basij bases and checkpoints across Tehran including Kamraniyeh, Enghelab, Azadi, and Fatemi districts.
The People's Mojahedin Organization of Iran (PMOI/MEK) conducted a coordinated operation targeting the Supreme Leader's compound in Tehran.
Approximately 100 MEK fighters were killed, wounded, or detained, while roughly 150 successfully withdrew from the operation against the Supreme Leader's compound.
Iran's government structure reportedly remains stable in 2026 despite the loss of the Supreme Leader.
A successful popular uprising in Iran remains improbable as the public is unarmed and unorganized despite regime military losses.

regime_fall.current_status: Following Ali Khamenei's death and Mojtaba Khamenei's succession, the Iranian regime faces catastrophic military degradation with over 5,000 targets struck, 85%+ air defenses destroyed, key security leadership assassinated, and unprecedented internal instability including citizen intelligence sharing and MEK operational activity against regime targets.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Israel conducted extensive strikes on March 15-16, 2026, targeting over 200 locations including nuclear infrastructure, IRGC/Basij headquarters, 100+ production facilities, and 500+ command and control targets, while Israeli and US forces assassinated senior security officials Ali Larijani and Gholamreza Soleimani on March 17.
